favorites.dial: white-indigo guilloche, sensu PR, onyx crown cabochon
favorites.note: #1 want. Recent LoupeThis sold $3,520 (Mar) / $3,960 (Apr). Target sub-$2,500.

favorites.dial: German silver, hand-engraved Romans, 18k gold moon
favorites.note: Apply May 18-21 ORDER tab. Type 3A discontinued at 15pcs; check 2026 successor refs.
Salon-only pickup at Kurono Aoyama. Email info@kuronotokyo.com to inquire before trip.
Very rare on secondary. Set search alerts.
Hits the muted green/warm-tone sweet spot.
Smallest case in the Kurono family — perfect.
Most affordable of the small-case favorites.
Sold out Jan 15. Deliveries from late Feb 2026 → secondary listings appearing now. Best June-buy candidate online.

brands.name: Kurono Tokyo
brands.style: Vintage-inspired dress watches, art-deco / 1930s-50s, urushi & guilloche dials
brands.note: Sub-brand of Hajime Asaoka. Sweet spot for the profile. Aoyama salon is must-visit.

brands.name: Naoya Hida & Co.
brands.style: Neo-classical, German silver dials, hand-engraved Romans, tourist-blue hands
brands.note: 75 watches/year. 25 to Armoury, 40 direct, 5 to Kamine Kobe. Apply May 18-21 lottery.

brands.name: Hajime Asaoka
brands.style: Tsunami, Project T tourbillon — atelier flagship pieces
brands.note: Parent atelier of Kurono. Tsunami in steel ~$30k. Tourbillons six figures.

brands.name: Kikuchi Nakagawa
brands.style: Murakumo & Ichimonji — black-polished, ultra-minimal, swordsmith-trained finishing
brands.note: Murakumo waitlist 7+ years and currently closed. Ichimonji similar. View at trunk shows / A Collected Man.

brands.name: Masa & Co (Masa's Pastime)
brands.style: Sterling silver Bombay dials, MP1 in-house movement, anonymous case
brands.note: Nagi & Sohkoku. Lottery system. Way over budget but worth seeing in person if possible.

brands.name: Minase
brands.style: Divido & 7 Windows — obsessive case finishing, urushi & yusai handcrafted dials
brands.note: Divido is 40.6mm — too big per profile. 7 Windows at 38mm is the closer fit.

brands.name: Daizoh Makihara
brands.note: Independent atelier. Pieces by commission / occasional retail.

brands.name: Quiet Club (Norifumi Seki)
brands.note: Very small output. Worth tracking via Instagram for occasional drops.

brands.name: Kazuo Maeda
brands.style: GPHG-nominated debut. Highly traditional Japanese craft.
brands.note: Just-emerged maker. Verify availability before trip.

brands.name: Vague Watch Co.
brands.note: Entry-level vibe / fun stocking-stuffer pick. Not a primary target.

sellers.name: Chrono24
sellers.best_for: Largest dealer marketplace. Best for Kurono Shiraai, Inseki, Mori
sellers.note: Set saved searches with brand + max price. Dealers vary widely on condition transparency.

sellers.name: WatchCharts
sellers.best_for: Price history, market index, sold comps
sellers.note: Use to validate ask prices. WatchCharts Marketplace has lower seller fees than Chrono24.

sellers.name: EveryWatch
sellers.best_for: Aggregates dealer + auction listings into one search
sellers.note: Cross-checks Chrono24, LoupeThis, Phillips, dealer websites in one view.

sellers.name: LoupeThis
sellers.best_for: Weekly online auctions, often Kurono / indie pieces
sellers.note: Recent Shiraai sold $3,520 (Mar 2026) / $3,960 (Apr 2026). Set max bids.

sellers.name: Bezel
sellers.fees: Buyer side included; price premium vs Chrono24
sellers.note: Cleaner experience but pricier. Good for first-time independent buys.

sellers.name: Wristcheck
sellers.best_for: Independents incl. Kurono, Naoya Hida secondary
sellers.note: Strong on Asian independents. Has physical Hong Kong showroom (see Stores).

sellers.name: Phillips
sellers.best_for: Naoya Hida, Hajime Asaoka, Kikuchi Nakagawa lots
sellers.note: Hong Kong & Geneva sessions regularly include Japanese independents.

sellers.name: A Collected Man
sellers.best_for: Hajime Asaoka, Kikuchi Nakagawa, NH curated single-owner sales
sellers.note: Best curated independent dealer in Europe. Pricey but vetted.

sellers.name: Hairspring
sellers.note: Smaller boutique dealer, occasional rare Japanese pieces.

sellers.name: Mr Watchley
sellers.note: Specializes in indie listings. Worth saving for NH alerts.

cities.name: Tokyo
cities.note: Heart of the Japanese independent scene. 3-4 days here gets every must-visit.

cities.name: Kobe
cities.note: Worth a Kobe day-trip from Kyoto/Osaka if you're chasing a Naoya Hida — Kamine is one of only three NH retail channels (5 pieces/yr).

cities.name: Kyoto
cities.note: Less density of indie watch retail than Tokyo, but Kuoe workshop and Kazuo Maeda's emerging atelier are based here. Good craft-tourism context.

cities.name: Osaka
cities.note: Komehyo + a handful of vintage shops. Lower priority unless already in town.

cities.name: Hong Kong
cities.note: Largest Asian retail hub for Japanese independents outside Japan. The Armoury HK is THE Naoya Hida destination.

cities.name: New York
cities.note: Armoury Tribeca is the US Naoya Hida channel. Trunk shows announced via newsletter.

cities.name: London
cities.note: Best Western entry point for Hajime Asaoka and Kikuchi Nakagawa pieces.

stores.name: Kurono Tokyo Aoyama Salon
stores.appointment: Walk-ins welcome; appointment recommended for try-ons
stores.note: Phone: 03-6823-8358. Gathering place > sales boutique. Email info@kuronotokyo.com to ask about Salon Edition allocation in advance.

stores.name: Naoya Hida & Co. Atelier
stores.address: Tokyo, Ningyocho, Chuo-ku (exact address by appointment confirmation)
stores.appointment: Required — request via naoyahidawatch.com or Instagram
stores.note: Pickup of allocated lottery watches happens here. Book early — slots fill.

stores.name: Shellman
stores.note: Oldest/most respected antique specialist in Ginza. On-site watchmaker for restoration.

stores.name: Antiqurious
stores.note: Specialty: classic dress + early Oyster Rolex. Mostly European but excellent eye for proportion.

stores.name: Watch CTI
stores.note: Equal weight on Swiss + Japanese. Good for vintage GS / KS even though those subbrands aren't your target.

stores.name: Nakano Broadway (3F watch dealers)
stores.note: Indoor mall — third floor is dense with tiny vintage watch shops. Spend an afternoon walking it.

stores.name: Dazzling
stores.note: Founded by the late Shigeharu 'Shiggy' Aritake. Highly curated rare European antiques.

stores.name: From Time To Times
stores.note: Small curated vintage space — worth a walk-in if nearby.

stores.name: Sekine Vintage
stores.note: Clothing-and-watches curator. Aesthetically aligned — quiet, minimal, considered.

stores.name: Jack Road (Nakano)
stores.note: Most likely place to find Kurono Shiraai or Inseki on JPY pricing. Bring passport for tax-free.

stores.name: Komehyo Shinjuku
stores.note: Big chain — quantity over curation. Occasionally has Kurono.

stores.name: GMT Watches Aoyama
stores.note: Boutique vintage shop. Aesthetically aligned.

stores.name: Kamine
stores.note: One of three Naoya Hida sales channels in the world. Worth a Kobe day-trip.

stores.name: Kuoe Kyoto Workshop
stores.note: Hand-assembled in Kyoto. Style is field/military — outside the dress profile, but the workshop is worth seeing.

stores.name: Komehyo Osaka
stores.note: Same model as Tokyo Komehyo. Skip unless already in Osaka.

stores.name: The Armoury Hong Kong
stores.note: Largest Naoya Hida channel globally. Trunk shows + standing allocation.

stores.name: Wristcheck Hong Kong
stores.note: Physical showroom in Central. Best in HK for secondary indie inventory.

stores.name: Phillips Hong Kong
stores.note: Spring & autumn watch sessions regularly include Japanese independents.

stores.name: The Armoury New York
stores.note: US channel for Naoya Hida. Subscribe to Armoury newsletter for trunk show dates.

stores.name: A Collected Man
stores.note: Premier Western dealer for Japanese independents. Curation is exceptional.

stores.name: Phillips London
stores.note: Use for preview viewings; major sessions are Geneva & HK.
